title: Verify or replace guild-toolkit repository references
description: 03-real-world-skills/01-the-workshop.md:11 points to github.com/Navigators-Guild/guild-toolkit and builds the entire Phase 3 Workshop chapter on the assumption that a populated Rust workspace exists there with named crates (guild-core, guild-progress, etc.) and open issues to claim. If that repository does not actually exist, or does not contain the described structure, Phase 3 Workshop is uncompletable — a novice will clone nothing (or a 404) and have no way to progress. Same concern for 03-real-world-skills/03-project-architecture.md:171 and 04-proving-it/02-giving-back.md:27 which reference reviewing PRs on the guild-toolkit. Fix: (1) Verify the repo exists and is public; (2) Verify it contains the crates the chapter describes and has onboarding-appropriate issues labeled for apprentices; (3) If it does not exist, either create a minimal seed repo with one or two starter tools and a CONTRIBUTING.md, or mark Phase 3 Workshop chapter as 'pending guild infrastructure' and provide a substitute exercise (e.g. contribute to any existing open-source Rust project of the apprentice's choice).
